title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox extended their winning streak to 11 games by sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Jarren Duran and Carlos Narvaez led the Red Sox to a 10-0 victory in Game 1 of the doubleheader.
Wilyer Abreu's two homers aided Boston's 5-3 win in Game 2, securing their place in the wild-card race.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early, both on the 15-day IL, show promising recovery signs and could return soon.
Tanner Houck, recovering from Tommy John surgery, is throwing off the mound twice a week, a positive development.
Other injured players, including Roman Anthony and Garrett Crochet, remain without recovery updates or return timetables.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida had three hits, including a home run, lifting Red Sox in Game 1 of doubleheader against Rays.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 over two games, consistently pressuring Tampa Bay's pitching staff to contribute offensively.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings in Game 1, helping preserve Red Sox bullpen for their successful doubleheader sweep.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Patriots coach Mike Vrabel amid offseason controversy, calls him infallible on ESPN's First Take.
Vrabel denied affair with reporter Dianna Russini; she resigned from The Athletic after an investigation.
Patriots start training camp next weekend, following a Super Bowl loss under Vrabel in his first coaching season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a new crime series streaming on Apple TV.
'Obsession,' a horror film grossing over $400 million, is streaming on Peacock.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople' featuring the late Sam Neill is available on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin violated NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman at a Concord convenience store in 2024.
Lufkin faces a $5,000 civil fine, $4,000 suspended, plus a restraining order, as per Merrimack County Superior Court.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to assault charges, sentenced to 24 months with only 6 served, linked to the 2024 incident.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox' trade deadline plans remain fluid, with potential moves considered before the Aug. 3 deadline.
Boston is on a 10-game winning streak, boosting their playoff odds to 44.3% according to FanGraphs.
Interest in acquiring a right-handed hitter exists, but the team has yet to make concrete decisions.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is for sale for $12.5 million, including the restaurant and staff housing.
Owners Evan and Maria Marley are selling Pi Pizzeria after 25 years to retire and seek a buyer to continue its tradition.
The property features a 46-seat dining room, wood fire pizza oven, and is actively hiring while a buyer is sought.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's World Cup team departed Boston after staying at the Four Seasons Hotel since early June.
The team is heading to Miami for a third-place match against England, following a semifinal loss to Spain.
France's squad practiced at Bentley University in Waltham during their stay and shared farewell images online.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced his production company, Artists Equity, is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges in the 2022 death of her boyfriend, Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe.
Other adaptations of the case include a Lifetime movie and an upcoming Prime Video series starring Elizabeth Banks.
